note:
7月下旬，美国短暂地被“来自中国的未经请求的种子”迷住了，这些种子开始出现在所有50个州的邮箱里。这些神秘的种子引发了美国农业部的警告，称人们不应该种植它们，而应该通知他们的州农业部门，并将它们邮寄给美国农业部或当地官员。
根据Motherboard从州农业部获得的文件，至少有数百甚至数千名美国人播下了种子。
自从种子故事最初曝光以来，我一直痴迷于了解更多。为此，我提交了52份信息自由申请；向所有50个州的农业部(或相当于州级部门)以及华盛顿特区和波多黎各各提交一份。我还向美国农业部及其几个实验室提交了申请。上个月，数以千计的电子邮件、电子表格、报告和文档以及语音邮件录音陆续涌入，它们在许多方面都很有启发性。
根据我读过的文件，神秘种子行动的规模比我最初怀疑的要大得多，也比最初报道的要大得多。保守地说，数以万计的美国人在7月份收到了他们认为是中国神秘种子的东西。一些州，如北卡罗来纳州，有超过1000人联系农业部，因为他们收到了主动提供的种子。其他地方，如新墨西哥州，大约有100个录制的种子接收器。这些种子接收者中的许多人，无论在哪里，都惊慌失措。
大约一个月前，我确实收到了来自中国的种子。我猜是中国，因为它看起来像中国文字。7月下旬，新墨西哥州一名妇女在留给该州农业部的语音信箱中表示：我想，哦，酷啊，也许伯吉斯种子或某家种子公司给我寄来了一些种子。而且，嗯，我像个笨蛋一样种下了它们，不知道有问题，新墨西哥州的一名妇女在一封留给该州农业部的语音信箱中说。现在，我已经和这个问题斗争了几个星期了。现在，我种植它们的地方，我记得我种植它们的地方，我种植它们的花园里的一切都很艰难，开始死亡…。我真的不知道现在该怎么办，所以谁能给我回个电话，给我一点指导吗？我知道我是个笨蛋。
像这样的电话并不少见。根据文件，一些人吃了种子。有人拨打了911。举行了紧急会议和电话会议。美国农业部的走私拦截和贸易合规组(SITC)、海关和边境保护局以及联邦调查局开始调查。
“是的，”SITC的一名官员大卫·斯特宾斯(David Stebbings)在接到新罕布夏州当局关于种子的警告后，通过电子邮件发送给他，“它开始爆炸了。”
在这个谜团的最初几天，许多州的农业部门都被居民的电子邮件和电话淹没了，他们不确定自己收到了什么，也不知道应该怎么处理种子。
密歇根州农业部法律事务办公室主任布拉德·迪肯(Brad Deacon)说，“我们的呼叫中心完全被电话压得喘不过气来。”“脸书上有五六百条帖子，直接留言…”
密歇根州农业和农村发展部发言人詹妮弗·霍尔顿(Jennifer Holton)在电话中告诉Motherboard，“人们正在种植它们，而且已经种植了它们。”“我们的工厂人员没能跟上电话。”
根据密歇根州编制的一份电子表格，677人向州政府提交了关于收到未经请求的种子的官方投诉；30人报告种植了种子。“我把它们种在家里的水培系统里，我以为它们是我从亚马逊订购的草莓种子。它们变成了黑色和绿色的霉菌，所以我把它们扔掉了，“一个人写道。“如果我知道这些种子来自中国，我就不会从亚马逊购买了。我还在等待至少另外4个订单的种子。如果他们来了就会烧死他们。“。
北卡罗来纳州编制的植物接收器电子表格显示，仅在该州，就有近1300人报告获得了种子。这些人中大约有60人种下了它们。
“收到很多船货。种植了一些，三叶草就长出来了，“一份报告说。“她表示是她种的球茎，”另一个州说。“种了一包，吃了长出来的牛至。还剩下一些，“还有一人说。一位种子接受者指出，他们在种植了一些种子包后“在地上撒了盐”。
并不是所有的种子都是主动提供的。一些人订购了种子，拿到了他们支付的钱，种植了它们，然后当中国神秘的种子文章开始出现时，他们变得担心起来。
我从中国买了一些种子。我只种了一棵。尽管有雨和风，它还是蔓延到了后院。因为它只有50件，所以真的是连着的。我设计了珍珠脉络膜。一位新墨西哥州居民写道，请过来看一看。
那个州的官员不知道该怎么做：我需要你的指导，因为他们重新种植了，而且正在传播？两个州农业部的雇员互相写信给对方。
“我收到了这个来自中国的包裹。不幸的是，我把信封翻了个底朝天。上面说它们是种耳环。我从亚马逊订购了洋葱，以为他们是从中国买的。我越看他们，他们甚至都没有接近洋葱，我只是觉得他们送错了东西。不，我没有种东西，也没有打开包裹。我应该怎么处理它们，“一位居民在一封电子邮件中写道。
在一次交流中，一位州昆虫学家和一位植物健康总监讨论了他们应该如何分类，并对一个说他们吃了种子的人做出了回应。
“我正在将它们添加到我的数据库中。当然，目前还不存在表明有人吃了这些种子的字段，“植物健康主管写道。“我不知道他们是否也吃了其他种子或包装，”昆虫学家回答说。
新罕布夏州也收到了来自警察的电子邮件，他们说他们接到了“忧心忡忡的居民”的电话，不知道该怎么办。在这些种子得到媒体关注的几天内，美国农业部动植物卫生检查局(USDA)与州农业官员召开了一次紧急电话会议，其中试图评估，由于“数量非常大”，实际上追踪所有种子包是否“可控”。
“这种情况有结束的迹象吗？”电话读数中的常见问题问道。他们写道：“现在确定情况是在扩大、趋于平稳还是在减少还为时过早。”
在浏览数千页有关种子的文件时，很明显，至少在最初的几周里，没有人知道是谁寄出了种子，它们来自哪里(除了“中国”)，也没有人知道种子邮寄运动的目标。
最终，官方的说法变成，这是一场“刷单”活动，将价值较小的物品发送给在线账户被盗的人，或者作为礼物发送给人。为了给经过验证的买家(因为名义上购买和使用产品的人权重更高)留下正面评价，你需要实际购买或接收过一件物品，因此通过接收种子，来自该账户或名字的评论权重将更高。(因为名义上购买和使用了产品的人，所以你需要实际购买或收到一件物品，因此通过接收种子，来自该账户或名字的评论权重将更高。
刷单的想法仍然是美国农业部和其他机构所说的，但至少在我看过的电子邮件中，很少有人谈到这个骗局是如何运作的，或者为什么会发生。这一活动似乎也比任何其他已知的刷单活动或任何其他种子邮寄活动都要大得多。
根据我从新罕布夏州获得的一份信息说明书，CBP表示，它在一年内截获了15000粒“来自世界各地”的种子。从仅向少数几个州提交的投诉数量来看，很明显，这场运动的目标人群远远超过1.5万人；甚至不厌其烦地填写在线表格或打电话给当局的人数似乎远远高于这个数字。
我的一些信息自由请求被引用积极执法调查的州拒绝了。我们知道FBI、CBP和USDA都还在调查中。
(顺便说一句，这个项目向我展示了不同机构和州对信息自由反应的广泛差异，这些机构和州都有自己的FOI法律。一些州以积极调查为由完全拒绝了我的请求，另一些州要求我大幅缩小范围，一些州严重编辑了个人信息。与此同时，新墨西哥州向我发送了超过1千兆字节的文件，包括未经编辑的语音邮件、电子邮件和照片。)。
我们还知道，根据一些文件中包括的犹他州植物实验室的分析，许多种子已经被识别出来，似乎没有什么害处。
我们的种子实验室已经鉴定出以下几种植物：玫瑰、苋菜(不是棕榈)、2薄荷、假马香油、自愈、胡枝子和红薯。我们的APHIS-PPQ植物卫生主管希望我们通过杀菌或焚烧的方式销毁种子，“分析种子的人写道。
新墨西哥州的一项类似分析发现，有些种子是有毒的杂草，在美国大量存在，但许多州禁止人们种植。这些植物包括牛眼雏菊和绿篱杂草。新墨西哥州还鉴定了洋葱、黄瓜、西红柿、萝卜、胡椒草、苜蓿、玉米、莴苣、冬青树和留兰香种子。
美国农业部还向各州提供了销毁种子的官方指南，其中包括在烤箱中烘焙它们，将它们窒息在两个带拉链的垃圾袋中，并将它们浸泡在漂白剂中。如果种子是种下的，美国农业部目前建议人们至少在一年内不要在该地区种植任何东西，并销毁任何碰巧在那里自然生长的植物。另一种官方认可的处理方法是将种子完全用管道胶带包裹起来。
有一件事我很清楚，通过阅读这些文件。美国人民似乎对这种性质的骗局没有特别充分的准备。
政府官员和科学家之间的电子邮件，他们正在处理一个困难的情况，似乎高效，专业，并适当谨慎。但来自普通公众的沟通令人担忧。即使明确告诉人们不要播种，人们也会播种。数以百计的人不知道他们是否订购过种子，也不知道如何检查。有人拨打了911。其他人吃种子。其他人点了一些具体的东西，从点菜的人那里拿到了他们点的东西，然后仍然惊慌失措。其他人则非常愤怒，他们不得不支付邮费将种子寄给政府。北卡罗来纳州的一位获奖者说：“我没有收到种子。我收到一个从中国寄来的可疑包裹，里面有一把勺子和一把叉子，我担心里面装满了Covid。“。
签署“色情通讯”，即表示您同意接收来自“色情通讯”的电子通讯，其中有时可能包括广告或赞助内容。
note_en:
In late July, America was briefly enthralled with “ Unsolicited Seeds from China,” which started showing up in mailboxes in all 50 states. These mystery seeds prompted warnings from the USDA, which said people should not plant them, and should instead alert their state agricultural authority and mail them to the USDA or their local officials.
  According to documents obtained by Motherboard from state departments of agriculture, at least hundreds, perhaps thousands of Americans planted the seeds.
  Since the seed story originally broke, I have been obsessed with learning more. To do this, I filed 52 freedom of information requests; one with each of the departments of agriculture (or their state-level equivalent) in all 50 states plus Washington DC and Puerto Rico. I also filed requests with the USDA and several of its labs. Thousands of pages of emails, spreadsheets, reports, and documents, as well as audio voicemail recordings, have been trickling in for the last month, and they have been enlightening in many ways.
  Based on documents I’ve read, the scale of the mystery seed operation was much larger than I had originally suspected and than was originally reported. Conservatively, it is safe to say that tens of thousands of Americans received what they perceived to be Chinese mystery seeds in July. Some states, like North Carolina, had more than 1,000 people contact the department of agriculture having received unsolicited seeds. Others, like New Mexico, had roughly 100 recorded seed receivers. Many of these seed receivers, regardless of location, panicked.
  "About a month ago, I did receive seeds from China. I guess China because it looks like Chinese writing. I thought, 'Oh cool, maybe Burgess seeds or one of the seed companies sent me some seeds.' And, umm, like a dumbass, I planted them, not knowing there was a problem," a woman in New Mexico said in a voicemail left with the state's department of agriculture in late July. "And now, I've been battling this for a couple weeks. Now, where I planted them, and I remember where I planted them, everything that's in the garden where I planted them are having a hard time and are starting to die … I really don't know what to do at this point, so could somebody call me back and give me a little bit of direction about this? I know I'm a dumbass."
  Calls like this were not unusual. Some people ate the seeds, according to the documents. Some people called 911. Emergency meetings and calls were held. The USDA’s Smuggling Interdiction and Trade Compliance group (SITC), Customs and Border Protection, and the FBI began investigating.
  “Yes,” David Stebbings, an officer with the SITC, emailed when alerted by authorities in New Hampshire about the seeds, “it’s starting to explode.”
    In the initial days of this mystery, the agricultural departments of many states were overwhelmed with emails and calls from residents who were unsure of what they’d gotten in the mail and what they should do with the seeds.
  “Our call center was completely overwhelmed with calls,” Brad Deacon, director of the office of legal affairs for Michigan’s Department of Agriculture, said. “There were 5-600 Facebook posts, direct messages …”
  “People were planting them and have planted them,” Jennifer Holton, a spokesperson for the Michigan Department of Agriculture and Rural Development, told Motherboard in a phone call. “Our plant person was not able to keep up with the calls.”
  According to a spreadsheet compiled by Michigan, 677 people filed official complaints with the state about the receipt of unsolicited seeds; 30 reported planting them. “I planted them in my hydroponic system in my home, I thought they were the strawberry seeds I ordered from Amazon. They turned Black and green mold, so I threw them away,” one person wrote. “If I had known these seeds were going to originate from China, I would not have purchased them from Amazon. I am still waiting on at least 4 other orders of seeds. Will burn them if they come.”
  A spreadsheet of plant receivers compiled by North Carolina shows that in that state alone, nearly 1,300 people reported getting the seeds. About 60 of those people planted them.
  “Received many shipments. Planted some and clover came up,” one report says. “She indicated that she planted the bulbs,” another states. “Planted one pack and ate the oregano that grew. Has some left,” says yet another. One seed recipient noted that they “salted ground” after planting some of their seed packet.
  Not all of the seeds were unsolicited. Some people ordered seeds, got what they paid for, planted them, then became concerned when the Chinese mystery seeds articles began popping up.
  "I have purchased some seeds from china. I have only planted one. Although with the rain and wind, it spread through the backyard. Which is really wired since it only was 50 pieces. I plsnted the pearls chorophytum. Please come and take a look," a New Mexico resident wrote.
  Officials in that state weren't sure what to do: "I need your guidance for this one since they're planted, and spreading?," two state department of agriculture employees wrote to each other.
  “I received this package from China. Unfortunately I through the envelope away. It said they were stud earrings. I had ordered onions from amazon and thought they got them from China. The more I looked at them they don’t even come close to onions I just figured they sent the wrong thing. No I didn’t plant or open the package. What should I do with them,” a resident wrote in an email.
  In one exchange, a state entomologist and a plant health director discuss how they should categorize and respond to a person who said they had eaten the seeds.
  “I’m adding them to my database. Of course, there doesn’t yet exist field to indicate that someone ATE the seeds,” the plant health director wrote. “I don’t know if they also ate other seeds or the packaging,” the entomologist responds.
  New Hampshire also got emails from cops, who said that they were getting calls from “concerned residents” and didn’t know what to do. Within a few days of the seeds getting media attention, an emergency call was held by the USDA’s Animal and Plant Health Inspection Service with state agricultural officials in which it attempted to, among other things, assess whether actually tracking all of the seed packets was “manageable” due to the “very high volume.”
  “Is there an end to this situation in sight?” an FAQ in a readout from that call asked. “It is too early to determine whether the situation is expanding, leveling off, or decreasing,” they wrote.
    While scanning through thousands of pages of documents about the seeds, it became clear that, for at least the first few weeks, no one had any idea who sent the seeds, where they came from (other than “China”), or the goal of the seed mailing campaign.
  Eventually, the official line became that this was a “brushing” campaign, in which items of small value are sent to people whose online accounts have been compromised, or are sent to people as a "gift." In order to leave a positive review from a "verified buyer" (which is weighted higher because the person nominally bought and used the product), you need to have actually bought or received an item, so by receiving seeds, reviews from that account or name will be weighted higher.
  The "brushing" idea is still what USDA and other agencies are saying, but, at least in the emails I’ve reviewed there’s very little talk about how the scam worked or why it happened. This campaign also seems to be much larger than any other known brushing campaign or any other seed mailing campaign.
  CBP says that it intercepts 15,000 seeds “from all sources worldwide” in a year, according to an information sheet I obtained from New Hampshire. It is obvious from the number of complaints filed to just a handful of states that this campaign targeted far more than 15,000 people; the number who even bothered to fill out an online form or call the authorities seems to be significantly higher than that.
  Some of my freedom of information requests have been rejected by states who cite an active law enforcement investigation. We know that the FBI, CBP, and USDA are all still investigating.
  (As an aside, this project has shown me the wide variability of freedom of information responses across agencies and states, which have their own individual FOI laws. Some states wholly rejected my request citing active investigations, others asked me to narrow it significantly, some heavily redacted personal information. New Mexico, meanwhile, sent me more than a gigabyte of files, including unredacted voicemails, emails, and photographs.)
  We also know, based on analysis from a Utah plant lab that was included in some of the documents, that many of the seeds have been identified and do not seem harmful.
  “Our seed lab has identified the following: rose, amaranth (not Palmer), 2 mints, False Horse Balm, Self Heal, Lespedeza and Sweet Potato. Our APHIS-PPQ plant health director wants us to destroy the seeds by sterilization or incineration,” the person who analyzed them wrote.
  A similar analysis in New Mexico found that some of the seeds were "noxious weeds" that exist in huge numbers in the United States but which people are banned from planting in many states. Those include oxeye daisy, and hedge bindweed. New Mexico also identified onion, cucumber, tomato, radish, peppergrass, alfalfa, corn, lettuce, hollyhock, and spearmint seeds.
  The USDA also gave states an official guide to destroying the seeds, which included baking them in an oven, suffocating them in two ziplock trash bags, and soaking them in bleach. If the seeds were planted, the USDA is currently advising people not to plant anything in that area for at least one year, and to destroy any plants that happen to grow there naturally. Another officially endorsed disposal method is encasing the seeds entirely in duct tape.
  One thing is clear to me, from reading these documents. American people do not seem particularly well-prepared for scams of this nature.
  The emails between public officials and scientists, who were dealing with a difficult situation, seem efficient, professional, and appropriately cautious. But communication from the general public is concerning. People planted seeds even when expressly told not to. Hundreds of people had no idea whether they had ever ordered seeds, or how to check. Some people called 911. Others ate the seeds. Others ordered something specific, got what they ordered from who they ordered it from, then still panicked. Others were furious they had to pay for postage to send the seeds to the government. From one recipient in North Carolina: “I did not receive seeds. I received a suspicious package from China with a spoon and a fork in it my concerns are that it is full of Covid.”

title: 数百名美国人播下了“中国神秘种子”
title_en: Hundreds of Americans Planted 'Chinese Mystery Seeds'

note:
华为宣布了其HarmonyOS操作系统的第二版，并详细计划将其引入更广泛的设备，包括智能手机。消费者业务首席执行官余承东今天在中国深圳举行的华为开发者大会上宣布了这一消息。
华为将于今天向开发者提供HarmonyOS2.0SDK的测试版，尽管最初它只支持智能手表、车载云台和电视。智能手机版的SDK将于2020年12月发布，Yu暗示运行HarmonyOS的手机可能会在明年出现。
华为还启动了OpenHarmony项目，该项目允许开发者在开源版本的操作系统上构建-类似于AOSP对于Android的意义。到今天为止，该项目只支持RAM为128MB或更低的设备，但明年4月将扩展到4 GB，到2021年10月将完全取消内存限制。
HarmonyOS对华为来说是一个具有重要战略意义的项目，因为它可以成为抵御禁止这家中国巨头与美国公司做生意的制裁的堡垒。华为目前被迫在没有谷歌服务的情况下出货基于Android的手机，这削弱了其面向中国以外用户的应用生态系统和功能。
note_en:
Huawei has announced the second version of its HarmonyOS operating system and detailed plans to bring it to a wider range of devices, including smartphones. Consumer business CEO Richard Yu made the announcement today at Huawei’s developer conference in Shenzhen, China.
 Huawei will make a beta version of the HarmonyOS 2.0 SDK available to developers today, though it’ll initially only support smartwatches, car head units, and TVs. A smartphone version of the SDK will follow in December 2020, and Yu hinted that phones running HarmonyOS might appear next year.
  Huawei is also kicking off its OpenHarmony project, which allows developers to build upon an open-source version of the OS — similar to what AOSP is to Android. As of today the project only supports devices with 128MB of RAM or below, but that’ll expand to 4GB in April of next year, and the memory limit will be removed completely by October 2021.
 HarmonyOS is a strategically important project for Huawei, as it could serve as a bulwark against sanctions prohibiting the Chinese giant from doing business with American companies. Huawei is currently forced to ship its Android-based phones without Google services,  crippling its app ecosystem and functionality for users outside China.
title: 华为HarmonyOS即将进军智能手机
title_en: Huawei's HarmonyOS is Coming To Smartphones

新平台需要学校电子邮件地址(@.edu)才能加入，并将位于Facebook应用程序的专用部分内。它可以从屏幕底部的选项卡中访问，或者从“更多”菜单旁边的“手表”、“约会”、“游戏”、“新闻”、“市场”等部分访问。
Facebook校园产品经理Charmaine Hung解释说：“我们想要创造一款产品，让同学们可以轻松地见面，培养新的关系，也可以轻松地开始对话，”Facebook校园产品经理夏曼·洪(Charmaine Hung)解释说。“我们真的认为校园现在比以往任何时候都更相关。在新冠肺炎身上，我们看到许多学生在秋季不会回到校园。现在，课程都在网上进行，学生们试图对这种新的常态做出反应，当你们不在一起的时候，联系到你们关心的俱乐部和组织是什么感觉。“她补充道。
更广泛地说，Facebook可能想要解决它的“青少年问题”，而Facebook校园是它的解决方案。
据报道，Facebook已经失去了对年轻群体的控制，因为他们已经将注意力转移到了其他社交应用上，比如YouTube，Snapchat和Instagram。皮尤研究中心(Pew Research Center)2018年的一项研究显示，在13岁至17岁的美国用户中，只有51%的人表示他们使用Facebook，低于2015年的71%。与此同时，爱迪生研究公司(Edison Research)2019年的一项调查显示，自2017年以来，Facebook已经失去了1500万用户，其中降幅最大的是12岁至34岁的人群。
Facebook校园的建立是为了通过在Facebook内提供一个更专属的私人网络空间来吸引这些用户回来。在某些方面，这类似于Facebook通过Facebook Workplace解决企业用户需求的努力。这些平台不是社交网络的新创意，而是利用Facebook的现有技术，如News Feed和Group，为特定人群提供解决方案。
Facebook Campus刚推出时，只能在全美约30所学院和大学使用(见下面的完整列表)，但该公司计划随着时间的推移进行扩张。
一些大学与Facebook建立了更深层次的合作关系，并签约将更新和新闻发布到学生的Facebook校园feed上。在这些情况下，学院或大学可能也会鼓励各种学生领袖加入网络。
Facebook将在其应用程序内和线下营销这款新应用程序。如果Facebook有足够的数据表明他们很可能是一所受支持的大学的学生，可能会通过新闻源中的提示提示学生加入校园。例如，如果Facebook用户定期访问受支持大学的Facebook页面，Facebook可能会显示Facebook校园注册提示。还有学生主导的激励计划，增加招生的学生将获得Facebook校园礼品，如t恤和毛巾。
除了需要一个.edu电子邮件地址，Facebook校园还需要一个毕业年-而且离现在不需要超过五年的时间。目前，Facebook不会在毕业后删除校园用户，但这些用户的参与方式可能会在未来进行修改。该公司表示，校友在大学社区中扮演着重要角色，但它仍在评估如何最好地将他们整合到未来的产品中。试点将帮助Facebook做出其中的一些决定，但还没有什么是板上钉钉的。
注册后，学生创建他们的Facebook校园个人资料。虽然这是在后台链接到学生的Facebook主要个人资料，但它允许他们添加特定于大学的详细信息，这些详细信息不会自动出现在Facebook的其他任何地方。在这里，校园用户可以添加他们的毕业年份、宿舍、主修和辅修、他们正在上的课程、家乡、Instagram个人资料等。
此信息只能由同一所学校的其他Facebook校园用户查看(除非这些用户已在Facebook上被屏蔽)。它还有助于增强校园的学生目录，Facebook校园用户可以在其中按姓名、年级、专业或班级搜索学生，或者浏览查找同学，将其添加为朋友，包括那些住在同一宿舍或俱乐部的同学。
在Facebook校园内，学生还可以发现并加入学校的群组和活动。这些可以是与官方学生俱乐部或希腊组织相关的，也可以是与特定宿舍相关的，甚至是那些只关注特定兴趣的组织，比如摄影、烹饪、写作、徒步旅行等。学生们也可以创建买卖群或室友搜索群，或者任何其他不违反Facebook条款的活动。
这些群组和活动基本上与Facebook上的功能相似，不同之处在于它们只能由学生查看、加入和访问。
Facebook校园也有自己的私人聊天区，与Facebook和Messenger分开。这些群聊的工作方式略有不同，因为用户实际上不必查找和邀请成员。取而代之的是，特定组中的学生可以选择加入其关联的聊天，如果他们愿意的话。
您的团体、俱乐部和活动的所有更新都在Facebook校园新闻源中。但与Facebook本身不同的是，学生不能在校园内发布个人资料。他们只能在群组、活动或聊天上发帖。
Facebook表示，这一决定有助于减少垃圾邮件，并允许用户将精力集中在他们参与的较小社区中。
少数几所大学已经与Facebook合作，在他们的Facebook校园频道上发布公告，让他们的学生看到。但是，任何学校都可以在发布时选择加入此功能。
本尼迪克特学院；布朗大学；加州理工学院；威廉与玛丽学院；杜克大学；佛罗里达国际大学；佐治亚南方大学；佐治亚州立大学；约翰霍普金斯大学；莱恩学院；林肯大学(宾夕法尼亚州)；米德尔伯里学院；新泽西理工学院；西北大学；赖斯大学；萨拉·劳伦斯学院；斯克里普斯学院；史密斯学院；斯佩尔曼学院；斯蒂芬·F·奥斯汀州立大学；塔夫茨大学；奥尔巴尼州立大学；纽约州立大学。
虽然Facebook的早期目标是常春藤盟校，但该公司表示，这些首批Facebook校园学校是出于多样性的考虑而选择的。即学生人口的多样性、地理的多样性和学校专业(如文科)的多样性。它们也代表着公立和私立学校的混合。
值得注意的是，Facebook校园将不会在其Feed中包含广告。但它将支持Facebook的广告业务。广告商不能特别针对Facebook校园用户，但他们可以通过兴趣来定位用户-即使用户表示他们感兴趣的唯一地方是校园内。例如，加入Facebook校园烹饪俱乐部的用户可能会成为希望接触到对烹饪感兴趣的用户的广告商的目标。
洪磊说，Facebook在今天之前没有测试过Facebook Campus，即使是在小团体中也是如此。相反，这次发布被认为是新体验的试点。然而，该公司确实花了时间与大学和学生团体举行圆桌会议，以获得产品洞察力和反馈。
note_en:
The new platform requires a school email address (@.edu) to join and will live within a dedicated section of the Facebook app. It will be accessible from a tab at the bottom of the screen or from the “More” menu alongside sections like Watch, Dating, Gaming, News, Marketplace and others.
 “We wanted to create a product where it was easy for classmates to meet each other, foster new relationships and also easily start conversations,” explains Facebook Campus Product Manager Charmaine Hung. “And we really think that Campus is more relevant than ever right now. With COVID-19, we see that many students aren’t returning to campus in the fall. Now, classes are being held online and students are trying to react to this new normal of what it’s like to connect to clubs and organizations that you care about, when you’re not together,” she added.
  More broadly, Facebook likely wanted to address its “teen problem,” and Facebook Campus is its solution.
  Facebook, according to reports, has been losing its grip on the younger demographic, as they’ve  shifted their attention to other social apps, like YouTube, Snapchat and Instagram. According to a 2018  study from the Pew Research Center, only 51% of U.S. users ages 13 through 17 said they used Facebook, down from 71% who said the same in 2015. Meanwhile, a 2019  survey by Edison Research indicated that Facebook had lost 15 million users since 2017, with the biggest drop coming from the 12 to 34-year-old group.
 Facebook Campus is built to bring these users back by offering a more exclusive place for private networking within Facebook. It’s similar, in some ways, to Facebook’s effort to address the needs of corporate users with Facebook Workplace. Instead of being new ideas for social networking, these platforms leverage Facebook’s existing technology, like News Feed and Groups, to deliver solutions for particular demographics.
 At launch, Facebook Campus is only available at around 30 colleges and universities across the U.S. (see full list below), but the company plans to expand over time.
 Some of the colleges have a deeper partnership with Facebook and have signed up to publish updates and news to their students’ Facebook Campus feed, as well. In these cases, the college or university may encourage various student leaders to join the network, too.
  Facebook will market the new app both within its app and offline. Students may be prompted to join Campus through a prompt in News Feed if Facebook has enough data to indicate they’re likely a student at a supported college. For example, if a Facebook user regularly visited a supported university’s Facebook Page, Facebook may display the Facebook Campus sign-up prompt. There are also student-led incentive programs where students who increase enrollment are rewarded with Facebook Campus swag, like t-shirts and towels.
 In addition to requiring a .edu email address, Facebook Campus requires a graduation year — and it will need to be no more than five years out from the present. For now, Facebook will not remove Campus users after graduation, but the way those users participate may be modified in the future. The company says alumni play a large role in college communities, but it’s still evaluating how to best incorporate them in the product further down the road. The pilot will help Facebook with some of these decisions, but nothing is yet set in stone.
 After signing up, students create their Facebook Campus profile. While this is linked on the back end to the student’s main Facebook profile, it lets them add college-specific details that won’t automatically appear anywhere else on Facebook. Here, Campus users can add their graduation year, dorm, major and minor, classes they’re taking, hometown, Instagram profile and more.
  This information can only be viewed by other Facebook Campus users who attend the same school (unless those users were already blocked on Facebook). It also helps power Campus’ student directory, where Facebook Campus users can search students by name, year, major or class, or browse to find classmates to add as friends, including those who are in their same dorm or clubs.
  Within Facebook Campus, students can also discover and join Groups and Events for their school. These can be those associated with official student clubs or Greek organizations, those associated with a particular dorm or even those just focused on a particular interest, like photography, cooking, writing, hiking, etc. Students can create buy/sell groups or roommate search groups, too, or anything else not in violation of Facebook’s terms.
 These groups and events essentially function like those on Facebook itself, with the exception being that they can only be viewed, joined and accessed by students.
  Facebook Campus also has its own private Chat section, which is kept separate from Facebook and Messenger. These group chats work a little differently, as users don’t actually have to find and invite members. Instead, students in a particular group can opt to join its associated chat, if they choose.
 All updates from your groups, clubs and events are in the Facebook Campus News Feed. But unlike on Facebook proper, students can’t post to their personal profile within Campus. They can only post to groups, events or chats.
  Facebook says this decision helps cut down on spam and allows users to focus their energy on engaging with smaller communities they’re involved with.
 A small handful of universities have already partnered with Facebook to distribute announcements to their Facebook Campus channel for their students to see. However, any school can choose to opt-in to this feature at launch.
  Benedict College; Brown University; California Institute of Technology; College of William & Mary; Duke University; Florida International University; Georgia Southern University; Georgia State University; Johns Hopkins University; Lane College; Lincoln University (Pennsylvania); Middlebury College; New Jersey Institute of Technology; Northwestern University; Rice University; Sarah Lawrence College; Scripps College; Smith College; Spelman College; Stephen F. Austin State University; Tufts University; University at Albany – State University of New York; University of Hartford; University of Louisville; University of Pennsylvania; University of Wisconsin-Eau Claire; Vassar College; Virginia Tech; Wellesley College; and Wesleyan University.
 While Facebook’s early days saw it targeting Ivy League schools, the company says these first Facebook Campus schools were selected for diversity’s sake. That is, diversity of the student population, diversity of geography and diversity of school specialties (like liberal arts). They also represent a mix of public and private schools.
  Facebook Campus, notably, won’t include advertising in its Feed. But it  will support Facebook’s advertising business. Advertisers won’t be able to specifically target Facebook Campus users, but they can target users by interest — even if the only place the user indicated they had that interest was within Campus. For example, a user who joined a cooking club in Facebook Campus could be targeted by an advertiser looking to reach users interested in cooking.
 Hung said Facebook hasn’t tested Facebook Campus before today, even with small groups. Instead, this launch is considered a pilot for the new experience. The company did spend time conducting roundtables with universities and with student groups to gain product insight and feedback, however.
title: Facebook凭借仅面向大学生的社交网络Campus回归根基
title_en: Facebook Returns To Its Roots With Campus, a College Student-only Social Network

本周早些时候，随着Android 11的正式发布，谷歌也为规格较低的手机设计的精简版Android进行了一些更新。
对于Android 11(Go版)，最重要的升级只是总体上性能更快，谷歌声称Android 11 Go的应用程序启动速度将比Android 10 Go快20%。同时，说到短信，Android 11Go在通知托盘中也有专门的对话部分，这样你就可以在一个地方看到所有正在进行的文本，而不需要考虑具体的应用程序。
最重要的是，Android 11 Go还获得了谷歌基于手势的导航，就像普通的Android 11一样。你可以向上滑动回家，或者从两侧滑动进入返回，或者向上滑动并按住查看你最近使用的应用程序，而不是沿着屏幕底部的一排按钮。
随着数字隐私变得越来越重要，谷歌还为Android 11 Go提供了更细粒度的安全设置，包括一次性授予应用程序对相机、麦克风或GPS等硬件的访问权限。当涉及到你很长时间没有使用的应用程序时，Android 11 Go会自动重置应用程序权限，以防止你可能忘记的旧设置构成了你的安全。
在Android 11中可以做以前不能做的15件事。
经过几个月的测试，Android 11今天正式推出，首先是Pixel手机和设备。
多读。
但也许Android 11 Go最大的改变是，此前，Android Go版仅限于内存为1 GB的手机。然而，随着智能手机内存变得更便宜、更容易获得，Android 11 Go已经更新，可以支持内存高达2 GB的手机。谷歌声称，这一改变应该会让Android 11 Go设备比以前多运行三到四个应用程序。
此外，在Android 11Go手机上，配备了完整2 GB内存的手机实际安装Android所需的存储空间更少，为照片、视频和应用程序等其他内容腾出了近1 GB(900MB)的空间。
不幸的是，目前还没有消息说Android 11Go何时会出现在当前的Go手机或未来的手机上。
一副索尼有源降噪耳机，一台宏基超宽游戏显示器，一台贝尔金…。
多读
note_en:
Following the   official release of Android 11 earlier this week, Google also has some updates in store for the stripped-down version of Android designed for phones with lower specs.
    For   Android 11 (Go Edition), the most important upgrade is just generally speedier performance, with Google claiming that apps will launch 20% faster in Android 11 Go compared to Android 10 Go. Meanwhile, when it comes to messaging, Android 11 Go is also getting a dedicated section for conversations in the notification tray, so you can see all your ongoing texts in one place, regardless of the specific app.
 On top of that, Android 11 Go is also getting Google’s gesture-based navigation, just like vanilla Android 11. Instead of a row of buttons along the bottom of the screen, you can swipe up to go home, swipe in from either side to go back, or swipe up and hold to see your recently used apps.
   With digital privacy becoming increasingly important, Google is also giving Android 11 Go more granular security settings, including the ability to grant apps access to hardware like cameras, microphones, or GPS on a one-time basis. When it comes to apps you haven’t used in a long time, Android 11 Go will automatically reset app permissions to prevent old settings that you’ve probably forgotten about from comprising your security.
   15 Things You Can Do in Android 11 That You Couldn’t Do Before
 Android 11 is officially rolling out today after months of betas, first to Pixel phones and devices
 Read more
 But perhaps the biggest change for Android 11 Go is that previously, Go Editions of Android were limited to phones with 1 GB of RAM. However, with smartphone memory becoming cheaper and more accessible, Android 11 Go has been updated to support phones with up to 2 GB of RAM. Google claims this change should allow Android 11 Go devices to keep three or four more apps running in the background than before.
  Furthermore, on Android 11 Go phones that do come with a full 2 GB of memory require less storage space for the actual Android installation, freeing up almost a full gigabyte (900 MB) for other stuff like photos, videos, and apps.
 Unfortunately, there’s no word on when Android 11 Go will be appearing on current Go phones or future phones.

今年1月，当冠状病毒首次在中国传播时，中国博士生曾伟磊在他位于加利福尼亚州河滨的公寓里在线观看了大流行的展开。在离家几千英里的地方，在罕见的不满情绪涌入中国社交媒体之后，他疯狂地试图跟上危机的消息：焦虑的患者写下的封锁日记；医院人满为患的视频片段；向年轻医生李文良致敬。当他第一次警告公众警惕病毒时，曾因“散布谣言”而受到谴责(仅仅一个月后，新冠肺炎就去世了)。然后，不可避免的是，当中国审查员介入清理互联网时，曾鸣会回到他几天前刚刚访问过的一个链接，发现只有熟悉的404错误信息-表明该页面已经消失。
曾鸣很快发现，这些帖子并没有消失。许多人被保存下来，悄悄地藏在互联网一个意想不到的角落里：GitHub，世界上最大的开源软件网站。GitHub成立于2008年，于2018年被微软收购，在开发人员和程序员中很受欢迎，他们主要使用该平台来分享和众筹源代码。曾经常把它作为与大学同龄人在研究项目上合作的一种方式。但在疫情爆发后，他偶然发现数以千计的中国网民将gihub重新用作新冠肺炎的档案，与审查机构争先恐后地以新闻文章、医学期刊和个人账户的形式记录疫情。
注册我们的Longreads时事通讯，从“连线”获得最好的功能、想法和调查。
一个被称为“存储库”的协作项目被命名为#2020nCovMemory。它由来自世界各地的七名志愿者创立，内容包罗万象，从中国新闻杂志财新发表的调查报道到武汉作家方芳的日记，后者批评当地政府压制信息，最初未能就病毒向公众发出警告。另一个名为Terminus2049的存储库-以艾萨克·阿西莫夫(Isaac Asimov)的基金会系列中的一个星球命名-收集了一些敏感文章，否则这些文章在中国的长城防火墙后面是无法访问的，比如对去年12月首次发现这种病毒的医生艾芬的采访。今年2月，曾加入了一个名为2020nCov_Personal_Archives的存储库，将大流行期间的在线日记条目和公民日常生活的描述众包。“知道这些故事正被保存在某个地方，这让我感觉平静多了，”曾说。
在中国互联网上，脸书和推特等全球社交媒体平台被禁止，微信和微博等国内平台受到严格监控。但是，被一些中国互联网用户称为“中国言论自由的最后一块土地”的GitHub仍然可以访问。中国当局不能审查单个项目，因为GitHub使用HTTPS协议，该协议对所有流量进行加密。但他们也不愿完全禁止GitHub，因为它对中国科技行业来说是无价的。中国的开发者严重依赖开源社区；仅在2017年，就有超过69万中国用户注册了一个账户。在GitHub上的开源项目数量上，中国仅次于美国。屏蔽该网站的成本太高了。2013年，这样做的尝试遭到了广泛的抗议；谷歌中国业务前负责人李开复等科技行业领袖认为，封锁会“让国家的程序员脱轨”，并导致“竞争力和洞察力的丧失”。几天后，封锁被拆除了。
所有这些都使GitHub成为少数几个躲避审查者的避难所之一，也是一个在线抵制的平台。GitHub经常用于分发GreatFire等反审查软件。2019年3月，中国沮丧的科技工作者创建了一个名为996.ICU的GitHub存储库，以分享他们令人精疲力竭的工作时间表，众包了一份非法强迫员工每周工作60小时以上的公司的“黑名单”，并向政府各部委起草了要求改善工作条件的请愿书。(996.ICU这个名字是基于一个笑话，即中国科技工作者常见的996时间表-一周六天早上9点到晚上9点-会把你送到重症监护病房。)。作为回应，一群在美国的GitHub和微软员工表达了对这项运动的支持，并向微软请愿，要求确保996 ICU存储库保持“不受审查，对所有人都可用”。
加州大学圣地亚哥分校研究中国审查制度的教授玛格丽特·罗伯茨(Margaret Roberts)表示，该平台独特的弹性可以通过“数字激进主义的萌猫理论”来解释。互联网思想家伊桑·祖克曼提出的这一理论指出，如果一个网站将敏感的政治与广泛吸引人的流行娱乐(比如棒棒糖表情包)结合在一起，那么该网站的审查将更具挑战性，因为用户希望访问这只可爱的猫。“但在GitHub的案例中，”罗布
但GitHub可能很快就会帮助中国缓解这种紧张局势。去年12月，该公司宣布计划在中国开设一家独立的子公司。据英国“金融时报”报道，GitHub首席运营官埃里卡·布雷西亚(Erica Brescia)表示，在与中国政府的讨论中，该公司正计划“分阶段”扩张。一个独立的GitHub子公司可能会让中国政府享受开源平台的经济福利，并有能力审查它认为不可接受的项目。“中国境内的用户更容易成为中国政治审查和监视的目标，”公民实验室的互联网审查和监视研究员杰弗里·诺克尔(Jeffrey Knockel)说。
尽管GitHub的一位发言人最近表示，他们“目前没有在中国设立实体的计划”，但微软过去也曾做出过类似的决定。该公司已经在中国提供了必应(Bing)、领英(LinkedIn)和其他产品的审查版本。GitHub没有回应采访首席运营官的请求，也没有回复有关12月公告的后续问题。
美国外交关系委员会(Council On Foreign Relations)数字与网络空间政策项目主任亚当·西格尔(Adam Sgar)表示，该产品的任何潜在分支--根据国籍将用户分成两个平台，即中国版和美国版--都将是“走向互联网分裂的又一步”。我们看到越来越多的例子，从Zoom最近决定停止向中国用户提供直接服务，以及Byteance对其TikTok和豆音产品的明确划分。
GitHub在与中国打交道时面临的挑战提出了自谷歌(Google)近10年前撤出中国以来科技行业一直面临的问题：企业是否应该向中国政府的要求让步，以获得世界上大量的在线用户基础？
Knockel说：“有许多平台，如谷歌、Twitter或Facebook，选择了不遵守中国的审查和监控要求，代价是无法进入中国市场。”“(但)微软为保持进入中国市场，遵守中国审查和监督要求的历史由来已久。”
如果归结为这样一种伦理权衡--是将中国的新冠肺炎帖子从数字以太网中抹去，以换取获得中国的开放源代码，还是在保存集体历史和推进技术进步之间--微软会做出什么决定？会得到什么，又会失去什么？
今年4月，在武汉的封锁结束，病毒在中国得到控制后，#2020nCovMemory存储库消失了，创建者出于安全考虑将其删除，最初的链接现在产生了一个404错误。由于中国的“形势”，该团队决定暂停该页面的运营-可能是出于对他们的人身安全和政府报复的恐惧。事实上，根据发给家人的一份通知，本月晚些时候，北京的一名“2049终点站”撰稿人因“寻衅滋事”被警方逮捕，尽管目前尚不清楚他的被捕是否与GitHub存储库有关。
“我们只是在保存个人故事。这并不是说我们将自己定位为以任何方式对抗政府。“曾鸣说。
尽管如此，曾承认，他还是会感到紧张。“我猜我们都只是在玩边界球，”他说，这在中国是一个常见的短语，意思是在不越过界限的情况下尽可能接近允许的界限。什么时候个人变成了政治，档案变成了另类历史，保存变成了反抗行为？“我想你永远不会真正确切地知道那条线在哪里，”曾说。“你永远不知道他们什么时候会来找你。”
更新时间2020年9月10日下午1：00：这篇报道的前一个版本错误地说微软在2016年收购了GitHub。那是2018年。
note_en:
When the coronavirus first spread through China in January, Chinese PhD student Weilei Zeng watched the  pandemic unfold online from his apartment in Riverside, California. Thousands of miles from home, he frantically tried to keep up with news of the crisis, following the rare outpouring of discontent that flooded Chinese social media: lockdown diaries penned by anxious patients; video footage of overcrowded hospitals; tributes to Li Wenliang, the young doctor who was reprimanded for “rumor-mongering” when he first warned the public about the virus (and would die of Covid-19 only a month later). Then, inevitably, as Chinese censors stepped in to scrub the internet clean, Zeng would return to a link he’d visited just a few days earlier to find only the familiar 404 error message—indicating that the page had vanished.
 Zeng soon discovered that these posts were not gone. Many had been preserved and quietly tucked away in an unexpected corner of the internet: GitHub, the world’s largest open source software site. Founded in 2008 and acquired by Microsoft in 2018, GitHub is popular among developers and programmers, who use the platform mostly to share and crowdsource code. Zeng often used it as a way to collaborate with his university peers on research projects. But after the pandemic hit, he stumbled on thousands of Chinese internet users repurposing GitHub as a Covid-19 archive, racing against censors to document the outbreak in the form of news articles, medical journals, and personal accounts.
 Sign up for our  Longreads newsletter for the best features, ideas, and investigations from WIRED.
 One collaborative project, known as a “repository,” was named #2020nCovMemory. Founded by seven volunteers from around the world, it included everything from investigative reports published by Chinese news magazine  Caixin to the diary entries of Wuhan writer Fang Fang, who criticized the local government’s suppression of information and initial failure to warn the public about the virus. Another repository, called Terminus2049—named after a planet in Isaac Asimov’s  Foundation series—collected sensitive articles that were otherwise inaccessible behind China’s Great Firewall, such as an interview with Ai Fen, the doctor who first discovered the virus in December. In February, Zeng joined a repository called 2020nCov_individual_archives, to crowdsource online diary entries and citizens’ accounts of everyday life during the pandemic. “It made me feel much more at peace, knowing that these stories were being saved somewhere,” Zeng says.
  On the Chinese internet, global social media platforms like Facebook and Twitter are banned, and domestic platforms like WeChat and Weibo are strictly monitored. But GitHub, known to some Chinese internet users as the “last land of free speech in China,” remains accessible. Chinese authorities cannot censor individual projects, because GitHub uses the HTTPS protocol, which encrypts all traffic. But they are also unwilling to ban GitHub entirely, because it is invaluable to the Chinese tech industry. The country’s developers are heavily dependent on the open source community; more than  690,000 Chinese users signed up for an account in 2017 alone. China is  second only to the US in the number of open source projects on GitHub. Blocking the site would be too costly. An attempt to do so in 2013 was met with widespread outcry; tech industry leaders like Kai-Fu Lee, former head of Google’s China operations, argued that the block would “derail the nation’s programmers” and lead to a “loss in competitiveness and insight.” Days later, the block was lifted.
  All this has made GitHub one of the few sanctuaries from censors and a platform for online resistance. GitHub is frequently used to distribute anti-censorship software such as GreatFire. In March 2019, China’s frustrated tech workers created a GitHub repository called 996.ICU to share their grueling work schedules, crowdsource a “blacklist” of companies that have illegally forced employees to work more than 60 hours a week, and drafted petitions to government ministries to demand better working conditions. (The name 996.ICU is based on a joke that the 996 schedule common among Chinese tech workers—9 am to 9 pm, six days a week—will send you to the intensive care unit.) In response, a group of GitHub and Microsoft workers in the US expressed support for the movement, and petitioned Microsoft to ensure the 996.ICU repository remained “uncensored and available to everyone.”
  The platform’s unique resilience can be explained through “the Cute Cat Theory of Digital Activism,” says Margaret Roberts, a professor studying Chinese censorship at UC San Diego. The theory, posited by internet thinker Ethan Zuckerman, states that if a website pairs sensitive politics with broadly appealing, popular entertainment—say, lolcat memes—the website will be more challenging to censor, because users want access to the Cute Cat. “But in the case of GitHub,” Roberts says, “the Cute Cat just happens to be the world’s open source code.”
   For Chinese authorities, GitHub’s continued presence on the country’s internet poses a familiar dilemma: on one hand, online dissent must be controlled. On the other hand, they are massively invested in the Cute Cat. At the heart of this is the precarious balancing act that the government has been performing over the past two decades: Can it keep the internet just free enough to nurture economic growth but not so free that it opens the door to political instability?
  But GitHub may soon help the Chinese alleviate that tension. Last December, the company announced plans to open a separate subsidiary in China. According to  a report by the  Financial Times, GitHub COO Erica Brescia said that the company, in discussions with the Chinese government, was planning a “phased approach” to expansion. A separate GitHub subsidiary could potentially allow the Chinese government to enjoy the economic perks of the open source platform and the ability to censor projects it deems unacceptable. “Users inside of China would be more easily targeted by Chinese political censorship and surveillance,” says Jeffrey Knockel, researcher of Internet censorship and surveillance at Citizen Lab.
 Although a spokesperson for GitHub recently stated that they “do not have plans to set up an entity in China, at this time,” Microsoft has made similar decisions in the past. The company already offers censored versions of Bing, LinkedIn, and other products in China. GitHub did not respond to requests to interview the COO or reply to follow-up questions about the December announcement.
 Any potential forking of the product—the segregation of users into two platforms, a Chinese version and a US version, based on nationality—would be “another step towards the bifurcation of the internet,” says Adam Segal, the director of the Digital and Cyberspace Policy Program at the Council on Foreign Relations. We’ve seen increasing examples of this, from Zoom’s recent decision to stop offering direct services to China-based users, and Bytedance’s clear demarcation of its TikTok and Douyin products.
 The challenges that GitHub faces by engaging with China raises issues that the tech industry has faced since Google pulled out of the country almost a decade ago: Should companies give in to the demands of the Chinese government in order to gain access to a massive slice of the worlds’ online user base?
 “There are many platforms such as Google, Twitter, or Facebook that have chosen to not comply with Chinese censorship and surveillance requirements at the cost of not having access to that market,” Knockel says. “[But] Microsoft has had a long history of complying with Chinese censorship and surveillance requirements in order to maintain access to the Chinese market.”
 If it came down to such an ethical trade off—between erasing China’s Covid-19 posts from the digital ether in exchange for access to the country’s open source code, between the preservation of a collective history and the advancement of technological progress—what decision would Microsoft make? What would be gained, and what would be lost?
 In April, after the lockdown in Wuhan ended and the virus brought under control in China, the #2020nCovMemory repository disappeared,  taken down by its creators out of security concerns, the original link now yielding a 404 error. The team decided to suspend operations of the page, due to the “ situation” in China—perhaps out of fear of their personal security and government reprisal. Indeed, later in the month, a Beijing-based contributor to Terminus 2049, was  arrested by police for “ picking quarrels and provoking trouble,” according to a notice given to his family, although it is unclear whether his arrest was related to the GitHub repository.
  “We’re just saving personal stories. It’s not like we’re positioning ourselves as confrontational to the government in any way,” Zeng says.
 Still, Zeng admits, he gets nervous. “I guess we’re all just playing boundary ball,” he says, a common phrase in China that means getting as close as possible to the bounds of what is permissible without crossing the line. When does the personal become political, the archive become alternative history, and preservation become an act of resistance? “I guess you never really know for sure where that line is,” Zeng says. “You never know when they’ll come for you.”
 Updated 9/10/2020 1:00 pm ET: A previous version of this story incorrectly stated that Microsoft acquired GitHub in 2016. It was 2018.
title: 在中国，GitHub是一个视频信息自由言论区
title_en: In China, GitHub Is a Free Speech Zone for Covid Information

周三，旧金山居民醒来时看到了橙色的天空，就像是从世界末日中走出来的东西。人们在社交媒体上分享了一张照片，照片中的天空被该地区主要野火冒出的浓烟变成了朦胧的橙色。AClimate a用汽车上的污染传感器测量“超本地”水平的空气，对这一现象有一个解释。
旧金山湾区上空的橙色天空似乎与旧金山湾区空气质量管理区网站上关于污染水平的每小时建议不符，这些建议通常显示的是健康或中度的空气污染水平，除了早上奥克兰和旧金山上空的空气水平显示为不健康。
但烟雾和传感器数据之间的不匹配对AClimate a首席科学家梅丽莎·伦登(Melissa Lunden)来说是有意义的，她在接受VentureBeat采访时表示。她说，逆温层将污染的、冒烟的空气悬浮在至少几千英尺的空气中，防止它下降到我们呼吸的地面。
伦登说：“这就像一块夹心蛋糕，空气不能混合。”“今天这里的烟雾是从很远的地方运来的，最远的是俄勒冈州。”
我看到很多人在社交媒体上发布橙色天空的图片，随后建议他们离开该地区，以避免呼吸空气。但许多人和我一样，对当地传感器的令人放心的读数感到困惑。
测量整个湾区空气质量的传感器都在地面上，就在我们呼吸的水平上。该地区没有那么多这样的固定传感器，这就是为什么AClimate a派司机用谷歌街景电动汽车在该地区周围用传感器测量污染。与其他污染测量收集器相比，该公司通过这种移动方法收集了大量数据。AClimate a之前使用这些数据评估了大流行对圣地亚哥汽车出行和污染水平的影响。但是AClimate a的数据还没有被实时处理。
8月16日，当旧金山湾区及周边地区开始起火时，在一场有1.1万次闪电的反常风暴之后，大火迅速蔓延。四场大火仍然包围着旧金山湾区，本周，加利福尼亚州奇科附近又发生了一场新的大火，向空气中喷射了更多的火山灰。AClimate a的科学家们继续研究这些复杂的闪电火灾对空气质量的影响。
伦登重复了她在早些时候的一次采访中告诉我的话。在夏天的晚上，一层逆温层的凉爽的海洋空气从海洋吹进来，并被困在一层较暖的空气下。海洋层是稳定的，这意味着在这个低层和上面的空气之间没有空气交换。
当太阳出来加热地面时，这一层的高度增加，在离地面很远的地方和靠近地面的地方都有更多的空气混合。凉爽的太平洋和炎热的内陆地区之间的气压差异导致陆上风在上午10点左右开始积聚，到下午晚些时候变成强气流。当太阳落山时，晚间逆温层再次形成。
火灾的排放物通常出现在山上海拔较高的地方，烟雾会高高地升入大气层。到了晚上，这个烟雾层被困在逆温层上方，不会下降到地面。然而，随着这一边界层在整个上午不断增长，一直高高悬在地面之上的烟雾混合到了这一层中，地面上的浓度也随之增加。白天，直接排放的烟雾也更有可能混合到这一层中。随着风力的增强，这些烟雾空气被从湾区吹向东部。当太阳下山时，晚间逆温层的改造导致更清新的空气更接近地面。
空气的健康程度取决于你在哪里。如果你靠近火，冒烟的空气可能会进入较低的空气区域，污染你呼吸的空气，特别是如果你能看到灰烬的话。
这些观察到的模式对已经发生的事情是正确的，但如果风向改变，模式也会改变。当有疑问的时候，这是空气质量的一个很好的来源。你也可以使用Air Now按邮政编码检查空气的健康水平，但你可能不能完全相信这个估计，因为它是基于政府在固定地点运行的监管传感器，然后数据被推断到覆盖更大的区域。这并不是基于AClimate通过其汽车收集的细粒度数据，但这几乎是目前可用的最好的衡量标准。
伦登告诫说，你不能根据你闻到的气味来判断空气是否足够安全，可以散步。
伦登说：“你从火中闻到的气味就像散发出来的有机嗅觉化合物，这些东西在大气中非常活跃，几个小时后就会消失，但烟雾仍然在那里。”“只是闻起来不再有烟味了。它仍然可能有相当高的浓度。“。
当来自太阳的阳光没有真正到达地球表面时，就像周三发生的那样，地面上就没有太多的热量。伦登说，热量没有打破混合层，所以混合层一直存在。“我们很幸运，因为混合层的持续性和稳定性意味着烟雾不会在地面上向下混合。”
她补充说，“当谈到空气质量时，外表可能具有欺骗性。有时候你看不到它，但它就在那里。在这种情况下，我们认为我们看到了它，因为今天是如此疯狂的一天。但事实是，我们看到的是烟雾，它不会降下来。图表是黄色的。我甚至无法想象，如果我们现在头顶上的所有东西真的混合到地面上，我们会看到多么集中。“
是什么产生橙色的光？当阳光在中午直射到我们身上时，它不必穿过这么多层云或烟。但是在早晨或日落时，太阳光以一定的角度进入，穿过大量的烟雾，这会产生橙色的效果，因为一些光线被散射，一些光线穿透。
“这真的很像你在日落时看到的，”伦登说。“当你在世界上雾霾弥漫的地方看到云或日落时，那些日落是非常橙色的。”
根据光线波长的不同，烟雾粒子对太阳光的散射也会有所不同。通常情况下，空气中的气体分子会将蓝光散射到各个方向，而这个散射的蓝光就是我们所看到的。烟雾颗粒完全过滤掉蓝光，而波长更长的橙色和红色光进入我们的眼睛。
我看到很多人都在谈论即将上映的电影“沙丘”或“银翼杀手2049”中的阿拉基斯形象。许多人开玩笑说，这就是世界末日或核冬天的天空。
“”银翼杀手2049“有这种橙色的空气，我们知道沙漠空气可以是橙色的，”伦登说。“如果有风，而且会把沙子踢到空中，那就会把蓝天变成更橙色的天空。”
当上面的天空如此厚重时，可以透过的阳光就会减少，这意味着地面将比正常情况下更凉爽。如果地面空气比较凉爽，逆温层就会持续，坏空气就不会降临到我们这里。
伦登说：“每年的这个时候，由于我们所处环境的性质，比如在太平洋上，我们往往会相当频繁地形成逆温层。”
然而，过去几天旧金山湾区的炎热天气可以抵消这一点，因为炎热可以阻止来自海洋的寒冷天气，阻止冷空气进入内陆。
有没有逆温层坍塌、烟雾弥漫的空气下落的情况？是的，这是可能发生的，特别是如果夜间较冷的空气没有从海洋进入的话。如果上面有暖空气，下面有暖空气，空气就会更多地混合在一起，空气中含量较高的污染物就可以降下来。这并没有发生在周三，因为你可以从图表中看出，空气质量主要是绿色和黄色的。希望它能一直这样下去。
note_en:
San Francisco residents awoke on Wednesday to an orange sky, like something out of the apocalypse. People shared images on social media of a sky turned hazy orange by smoke coming in from major wildfires throughout the region.  Aclima, which measures air on a “hyperlocal” level with pollution sensors on cars, had an explanation for the phenomenon.
 The orange sky over the Bay Area didn’t appear to match the hourly recommendations about pollution levels on the Bay Area Air Quality Management District’s website, which generally showed healthy or moderate air pollution levels, except in the morning over Oakland and San Francisco, where air levels were shown to be unhealthy.
  But the mismatch between the smoky haze and the stats from sensors made sense to Aclima chief scientist Melissa Lunden, who spoke with VentureBeat in an interview. She said an inversion layer suspends the polluted, smoky air at least a couple of thousand feet in the air and keeps it from descending to the ground level where we breathe.
 “It’s like a layer cake where the air doesn’t mix,” Lunden said. “The smoke that is here today has been transported from a long way away, as far as Oregon.”
 I saw a lot of people posting images of the orange sky on social media, followed by suggestions that they should leave the region to avoid breathing the air. But many were puzzled, like I was, by reassuring readings from local sensors.
  The sensors that measure air quality throughout the Bay Area are on the ground, at the level we breathe. There aren’t that many of these fixed sensors in the region, which is why Aclima sends drivers around the area in Google Street View electric cars to measure pollution with sensors. The company gathers a massive amount of data compared to other pollution measurement collectors through this mobile method. Aclima previously used this data to  assess the pandemic’s effect on car travel and pollution levels in San Diego. But Aclima’s data isn’t processed in real time yet.
 When fires started in the Bay Area and surrounding regions on August 16, after a freak storm with 11,000 lightning strikes,  they spread fast. Four major fires still encircle the Bay Area, and this week a new blaze started near Chico, California that kicked more ash into the air.  Aclima scientists have continued to examine the impact of these lightning complex fires on air quality.
 Lunden repeated what she told me in an earlier interview. On summer evenings, an inversion layer of cool marine air blows in from the ocean and is trapped beneath a layer of warmer air. The marine layer is stable, meaning there is no exchange of air between this lower level and the air above it.
  As the sun comes out and heats the ground, the height of this layer increases, and there is more mixing of air both far above and near ground level. The difference in atmospheric pressure between the cool Pacific and the heating inland regions results in an onshore wind that starts to build mid-morning to a strong flow by late afternoon. As the sun sets, the evening inversion layer forms again.
 Emissions from the fires are often found at higher elevations in the hills, and smoke rises high into the atmosphere. In the evening, this smoky layer is trapped above the inversion layer and does not descend to the ground level. As this boundary layer grows throughout the morning, however, the smoke that has been held high above the ground mixes into the layer, and concentrations on the ground increase. Direct smoke emissions are also more likely to mix into this layer during the day. As the winds pick up, this smoky air is blown out of the Bay Area toward the east. And when the sun goes down, the reformation of the evening inversion layer results in clearer air closer to the ground.
 How healthy the air is depends on where you are. If you are near a fire, the smoky air may make its way into the lower region of air and pollute your breathing air, especially if you can see ash.
  These observed patterns hold true for what has happened, but if the winds change the patterns will too. When in doubt,  this is a good resource for air quality. You can also check the health level of the air by zip code using  Air Now, but you may not be able to entirely trust that estimate because it is based on the government’s regulatory sensors operating in stationary places, and the data is then extrapolated to cover a much larger region. It’s not based on the fine-grained data Aclima collects with its cars, but it’s pretty much the best measure available at the moment.
 Lunden cautioned that you can’t judge whether the air is safe enough to take a walk based on what you smell.
 “What you smell from a fire is like the organic olfactory compounds that get emitted, and that stuff is pretty reactive in the atmosphere and disappears after some number of hours, but the smoke is still there,” Lunden said. “It just doesn’t smell like smoke anymore. It could still have pretty high concentrations.”
 When the sunlight from the sun isn’t really getting to the surface of the planet, which is sort of what happened Wednesday, you don’t have much heat on the ground. The heat is not breaking the mixing layer up, and so the layer persists, Lunden said. “We are fortunate in that the persistence of that mixing layer and its stability means that that smoke is not mixing down at ground level.”
 She added, “When it comes to air quality, looks can be deceiving. Sometimes you can’t see it, and it’s there. In this case, we think we’re seeing it because it’s such a crazy day. But the fact is, what we’re seeing is smoke, and it’s not coming down. The charts are yellow. I can’t even imagine what concentration we would see if all of what we had on top of us right now actually mixed down to the ground level.”
   What produces the orange light? When sunlight comes straight down at us at noon, it doesn’t have to pass through so many layers of clouds or smoke. But in the morning or at sunset, the sun’s rays are coming in at an angle and passing through a lot of smoke, which produces the orange effect as some of the light gets scattered and some penetrates.
 “It is really very much like what you see at sunset,” Lunden said. “When you see clouds or sunsets in smoggy parts of the world, those sunsets are very orange.”
 Smoke particles scatter sunlight differently depending on the wavelength of the light. Normally, the gas molecules in the air scatter the blue light in all directions and this scattered blue light is what we see. The smoke particles completely filter out the blue light while the longer wavelength orange and red light gets through to our eyes.
   I saw a lot of people talk about the images of Arrakis from the upcoming film  Dune or the polluted skies of  Blade Runner 2049. Those are the skies of the apocalypse, or nuclear winter, a lot of people joked.
 “ Blade Runner 2049 had this orange air, and we know that desert air can be orange,” Lunden said. “If it’s windy and it’s kicking up sand in the air, it’s going to shift a blue sky to more orange.”
  When the skies above are so thick, less sunlight can get through, and that means the ground will be cooler than normal. If the ground air is cooler, the inversion will hold and the bad air won’t come down to us.
 “This time of year, we tend to have the inversion layer form pretty frequently because of the nature of our environment, like being on the Pacific Ocean,” Lunden said.
 The hot weather in the Bay Area over the last few days can counter that, however, as the heat can block cold weather coming in from the ocean and stop that cool air from making its way inland.
 Are there conditions in which the inversion layer collapses and the smoky air comes down? Yes, that can happen, especially if the colder air is not moving in from the ocean during the night. If there is warm air above the layer and warm air below, the air mixes together more, and the pollutants that are high in the air can come down. That didn’t happen Wednesday, as you can tell from the chart showing mostly green and yellow conditions for air quality. Let’s hope it stays that way.
title: 为什么旧金山会有世界末日般的橙色天空
title_en: Why San Francisco Had an Apocalyptic Orange Sky

Facebook今天正在回归其根源，推出了一款名为Campus的新产品，这是主要应用程序中专门为大学生设计的一个部分。校园设计为学生只能与其学校的同龄人互动；他们可以访问仅限校园的新闻订阅源，并加入与校园生活相关的群组、活动和群聊室(称为校园聊天室)。为了向最初的Facebook致敬，他们甚至可以访问一个“校园名录”，在那里他们可以找到其他学生并将其加为好友。
为了访问“校园”，学生们必须提供他们的.edu电子邮件地址和毕业年份，一旦他们进入，他们将专门为该部分创建一个新的个人资料，尽管他们通常的个人资料和封面照片将跟随他们进入“校园”。他们还可以选择添加或删除专业、班级、家乡、宿舍和辅修。他们添加的信息越多，他们就能找到更多有共同点的同学，当然，这也给Facebook提供了更多关于大学生学习什么以及他们在与谁交谈的数据，这为其广告定向决策提供了信息。Campus校长Charmaine Hung表示，该团队之所以选择单独的个人资料，是因为学生们可能不想在他们的公共资料中分享超具体的大学信息。
目前，Campus正在全美30所大学进行试点，包括约翰·霍普金斯大学、西北大学、路易斯维尔大学、瓦萨大学和弗吉尼亚理工大学。(不过，没有哈佛！)。这些学生将不能相互交流，来自附近大学的学生也不能。目前，校园是竖井状的，因此只有在同一所学校上学的人才能看到对方并进行互动。
尽管校园简介明显与Facebook本身隔开，但如果一名学生在他们正常的Facebook上屏蔽了另一个人，这一设置也将适用于校园，反之亦然。如果一个人在校园外违反了Facebook的社区标准，他们也不能加入这个部分。校友将被允许留在校园，尽管Facebook将发送一份通知，建议他们离开校园，因为这与他们没有太大的相关性。
校园可能是Facebook试图让学生和年轻人继续使用原来的Facebook应用程序并保持更长时间参与的一种方式。与此同时，它正在建立Facebook表示已经在该平台上看到的行为。学生们已经为他们的班级、宿舍和兴趣组成了小组，所以Facebook正在简化这一过程，并更多地控制学生如何找到彼此。尽管如此，有趣的是，Facebook并没有将Instagram整合到校园试点中，这实际上是将Facebook应用程序设置为校园中心，如果学生想找到同龄人，这是绝对必要的。
更新9/10，美国东部时间上午11：07：Facebook最初提供了一个包含占位符文本的图像；我们已经更新了主图像以反映实际文本。
note_en:
Facebook is getting back to its roots today with the launch of a new product called Campus, a section of the main app specifically designed for college students. Campus is designed to be a place where students can interact only with peers at their school; they can access a Campus-only News Feed and join Groups, events, and group chat rooms, called Campus Chats, pertaining to campus life. In a nod to the original Facebook, they’ll even have access to a “Campus directory” where they can find and friend other students.
 In order to access Campus, Students have to provide their .edu email address as well as their graduation year, and once they’re in, they’ll create a new profile specifically for the section, although their usual profile and cover photos will follow them to Campus. They’ll also have the option to add or remove their major, classes, hometown, dorm, and minor. The more information they add, the more they’ll be able to find classmates with commonalities, which also, of course, gives Facebook more data about what the college students are studying and who they’re talking to, which informs its ad targeting decisions. Charmaine Hung, PM of Campus, says the team opted for a separate profile because students might not want to share hyper-specific college information on their public profile.
  For now, Campus is being piloted with 30 universities around the US, including Johns Hopkins, Northwestern, University of Louisville, Vassar, and Virginia Tech. (No Harvard, though!) These students won’t be able to interact with each other, and neither will students from nearby colleges. For now, Campus is siloed so that only people who attend the same school can see and interact with each other.
 Although the Campus profile is notably walled off from Facebook proper, if a student has blocked another person on their normal Facebook, that setting will apply to Campus, too, and vice versa. If a person violated Facebook’s community standards outside of Campus, they also won’t be able to join the section. Alumni will be allowed to stay on Campus, although Facebook will send a notification that suggests they leave Campus because it won’t be as relevant to them.
 Campus might be one way Facebook tries to keep students and younger people on the original Facebook app and engaged for longer. At the same time, it’s building off behavior Facebook says it’s already seen on the platform. Students already form Groups for their classes, dorms, and interests, so Facebook is streamlining the process and taking more control over how students find each other. Still, Facebook interestingly didn’t build any Instagram integration into the Campus pilot, which effectively sets the Facebook app up as the central Campus focus and an absolute requirement if students want to find their peers.
 Update 9/10, 11:07 AM ET:  Facebook originally provided an image that contained placeholder text; we’ve updated the main image to reflect the actual text.
title: Facebook正在美国30所大学试点Campus，这是一个专门为大学生与学校同龄人互动而设计的部分
title_en: Facebook is piloting Campus, a section specifically designed for college students to interact with peers at their school, at 30 universities around the US

Bose准备用两款新产品重新进入竞争激烈的真正无线耳机市场。今天，该公司宣布推出QuietComfort耳塞(售价279美元)和运动耳塞(179美元)。(它们以前分别被称为噪音消除耳塞700和500。)。凭借旗舰产品QuietComfort耳塞，Bose承诺以真正的无线外形提供“所有Bose耳机的‘安静’”。至于运动耳机，本质上是2017年SoundSports Free的继任者，Bose表示，即使是最剧烈、最剧烈的锻炼，它们的设计也是为了舒适和稳定。这两款都将在9月29日开始供应，从今天开始预订。
因此，Bose正试图通过QuietComfort耳塞一举挑战AirPods Pro和索尼的1000XM3耳塞，而Sports耳塞将与Jabra Elite 75T和三星Galaxy Buds Plus等成本较低的产品展开竞争。(运动耳机没有任何类型的噪音消除功能。)。
但QuietComfort耳机肯定有。根据Bose的说法，你可以在11种噪音消除级别中进行选择。这种粒度在降噪耳机中并不少见，但在耳塞中更少见。每个耳塞的长度略高于一英寸，重0.3盎司--据Bose说，“大约相当于四分之一盎司”。耳尖被重新设计成更多的椭圆形，这显然有助于更好地密封。这种改进的噪音隔离与“一种独特的新算法”相结合，可以在不到一毫秒的时间内产生与外部噪音相反的信号。
结果是什么呢？“附近咖啡机的嗡嗡声逐渐消退为耳语，引擎和建筑发出的轰鸣声几乎消失了。”再说一次，当你记住我们谈论的是耳机的时候，这些都是很大的索赔。但Bose在这一领域有几十年的经验，围绕降噪技术有一长串的专利清单。我很兴奋地想看看这种体验是否符合市场营销的说法。这11个噪音消除级别的范围从完全透明到完全静音您周围的世界。在我的测试中，到目前为止，还没有一家公司能够超越AirPods Pro听起来自然的透明模式。
至于音质，Bose说，QuietComfort耳塞避免提升任何特定的频率，并致力于“忠实地再现”你的音乐，尽管该公司表示，低音“在任何音量下都是丰富和强劲的”。Bose说，在看视频或听播客时没有“电子嘶嘶声”；一些真正的无线耳塞可以发出可听见的、甚至令人讨厌的白噪声，但对于Bose的主要竞争对手来说，这不是问题。
电池续航时间还不错。QuietComfort耳塞可以连续收听长达6个小时，并从支持无线充电的手提箱中获得另外12个小时。6小时可以与AirPods Pro和索尼的1000XM3花蕾相提并论，尽管后者的机壳里有更多的果汁。QuietComfort耳塞将有黑色或“肥皂石”两种颜色可供选择，这两种颜色基本上都是灰白/银色。
根据Bose的说法，运动耳机是“从无到有的设计”-之前的SoundSports Frees听起来很棒，但存在一些连接和音频同步问题-并具有“一个全新的音响系统”。该公司表示，你可以在锻炼播放列表中期待强大的、鼓舞人心的音频，但运动耳机更多的是关于健身和舒适性。他们有一种“近乎神奇的挑衅来转移或闹翻”，这是我听过的最完美的、只有波斯才会说的话之一。运动耳机有黑色、白色(带有绿色口音)和蓝色。
它们的持续电池寿命在5小时内略短。这款机壳又增加了10个，而且没有无线充电。运动耳塞和QuietComfort耳塞在防水和防汗方面都被评为IPX4，所以健身耳塞在这方面没有任何优势；同样，它似乎更多的是关于适合性和在长距离跑步中不会造成任何不适的设计。
Bose说，这两对新耳塞都有蓝牙5.1“和一种新的数据传输方法，以减少辍学。”它们还拥有“即时触控功能，可以执行最常见的命令，如播放和暂停、接听和结束通话，以及访问您的个人助理。”我对书中没有提到的数量并不感到兴奋，但希望这是一个选择。这两组芽也都有自动暂停功能。使用QuietComfort耳塞，您可以拔下任一耳塞，您的音乐将会暂停，直到您将其放回耳塞。有了运动耳机，只有右边的耳机才有这个自动暂停检测功能。一次失望？这两款耳机--即使是售价279美元的QuietComfort耳塞--都不提供多点功能，这意味着你不能同时配对两台设备。这是Jabra超越竞争对手的优势所在。Bose非常擅长多点耳机，所以我希望耳塞也能做到这一点。
尽管如此，在经历了奇怪的长时间延迟之后，博斯已经带着一个有针对性的战略回来了。如果该公司能够利用其技术诀窍，并在噪音消除方面无可争辩地击败苹果和索尼，那么仅凭这一点就足以销售大量的QuietComfort耳塞-即使我们大多数人现在没有登上很多航班。至于运动耳机，这也归结于Bose能多好地兑现它的承诺。这家公司知道如何制造舒适的耳塞；仍然有人怀念停产的睡眠耳塞。但我认为Bose将更难在179美元的价格水平上脱颖而出。在接下来的几周里，请继续关注我对每一篇文章的评论。
note_en:
If you buy something from a Verge link, Vox Media may earn a commission. See our  ethics statement.
 Bose is ready to reenter the competitive true wireless earbuds market with two new products. Today, the company is announcing both the noise-canceling QuietComfort Earbuds,  priced at $279, and the  $179 Sports Earbuds. (They were previously known as the Noise Canceling Earbuds 700 and 500, respectively.) With the flagship QuietComfort Earbuds, Bose is promising to deliver “all the ‘quiet’ of Bose around-ear headphones” in a truly wireless form factor. As for the Sports Earbuds, essentially a successor to 2017’s SoundSport Free, Bose says they’re designed for comfort and stability through even the most vigorous, intense workouts. Both will be available on September 29th, with preorders starting today.
 So in one fell swoop, Bose is trying to take on the AirPods Pro and Sony’s 1000XM3 earbuds with the QuietComfort Earbuds, and the Sports Earbuds will compete with lower-cost options like the Jabra Elite 75t and Samsung Galaxy Buds Plus. (The Sports Earbuds don’t have any kind of noise cancellation.)
 But the QuietComfort Earbuds sure do. You can choose between 11 levels of noise cancellation, according to Bose. That level of granularity isn’t unusual to see in noise-canceling  headphones, but it’s a little rarer to find in earbuds. Each earbud is slightly over an inch long and weighs 0.3 ounces — “about the same as a quarter,” according to Bose. The ear tips have been redesigned with more of an oval shape, which apparently makes for a better seal. That improve noise isolation is combined with “an exclusive new algorithm” that produces an opposite signal to outside noise in under a millisecond.
   The result? “The buzz of nearby coffee grinders fade to a whisper, and the roar from engines and construction all but vanish.” Again, these are big claims when you remember we’re talking about earbuds. But Bose has decades of experience in this area and a long, long list of patents around noise-canceling tech. I’m excited to see if the experience matches the marketing speak. The 11 levels of noise cancellation run the gamut from full transparency to fully muting the world around you. In my tests so far, no company has managed to beat the natural-sounding transparency mode of the AirPods Pro.
   As for sound quality, Bose says the QuietComfort Earbuds avoid boosting any particular frequencies and go for a “faithful reproduction” of your music, though the company says bass is “rich and strong at any volume.” Bose says there’s no “electronic hiss” when watching videos or listening to podcasts; some true wireless earbuds can produce audible, even annoying levels of white noise, but it’s not an issue for Bose’s primary competitors.
   Battery life is decent. The QuietComfort Earbuds can hit up to six hours of continuous listening and get another 12 from their carrying case, which supports wireless charging. Six hours is right on par with the AirPods Pro and Sony’s 1000XM3 buds, though the latter two have more juice in their cases. The QuietComfort Earbuds will be available in either black or “soapstone,” which is basically an off-white / silver color.
   The Sports Earbuds were “engineered from a clean sheet” according to Bose — the previous SoundSport Frees sounded great but had some connectivity and audio sync issues — and feature “an entirely new acoustic system.” The company says you can expect powerful, motivating audio for your workout playlists, but the Sports Earbuds are more about fit and comfort. They have an “almost-magical defiance to shift or fall out,” which is one of the most perfect, only-Bose-would-say-something-like-that things that I’ve ever heard. The Sports Earbuds come in black, white (with green accents), and blue.
   Their continuous battery life is a little shorter at five hours. The case adds 10 more and lacks wireless charging. Both the Sports Earbuds and QuietComfort Earbuds are rated IPX4 for water and sweat resistance, so the fitness-geared buds don’t have any advantage there; again, it seems to be more about fit and a design that won’t cause any discomfort in the middle of a lengthy run.
 Bose says both pairs of new earbuds have Bluetooth 5.1 “and a new data transfer method for fewer dropouts.” And they’ve got “on-bud touch controls for the most common commands like play and pause, answer and end calls, and access to your personal assistant.” I’m not thrilled that volume goes unmentioned there, but hopefully it’s an option. Both sets of buds also have auto-pause. With the QuietComfort Earbuds, you can remove either earbud and your music will pause until you put it back in. With the Sports Earbuds, only the right earbud has this auto-pause detection. One disappointment? Neither pair — even the $279 QuietComfort Earbuds — offers multipoint, which means you can’t simultaneously pair to two devices at once. That’s an advantage that Jabra gets to hang over its rivals. Bose is very good at multipoint with headphones, so I wish it could’ve made the same happen with earbuds.
   Even so, after an oddly long delay, Bose has come back with a focused strategy. If the company can leverage its know-how and indisputably beat Apple and Sony at noise cancellation, that’ll be enough to sell plenty of QuietComfort Earbuds all by itself — even if most of us aren’t boarding many flights right now. As for the Sports Earbuds, those also come down to how well Bose can deliver on its promises. This company knows how to make comfortable earbuds; there are still people out there who miss the discontinued Sleep Buds. But that $179 price level is where I think Bose will have a harder time differentiating itself. Stay tuned for my reviews of each in the coming weeks.
title: Bose宣布售价279美元的QuietComfort耳塞，有11级噪音消除功能，以及179美元的运动耳塞，这两款耳塞都将于9月29日发售，今天就有预订
title_en: Bose announces $279 QuietComfort Earbuds, with 11 levels of noise cancellation, and $179 Sports Earbuds, both available on September 29th, with preorders today

现在Android 11已经下降，谷歌推出了Android 11 Go Edition，目的是加快入门级智能手机的速度。许多变化都是为了利用现代经济型设备上改进的功能，如更大的屏幕、双摄像头、更大的RAM和额外的存储空间。
根据谷歌的说法，该操作系统现在支持2 GB的RAM(之前为1.5 GB)，为用户提供了额外的270MB的可用空间-足以在后台运行“3到4个应用程序”。为此，它启动应用程序的速度可以比Android Go 10快20%，在不降低手机速度的情况下，可以更快地切换应用程序。2 GB设备上的Android Go版还支持高达900MB的额外存储，例如足以下载一部电影。
更新后的操作系统还具有新的便利和隐私功能。谷歌写道，它在通知部分的一个专用空间显示所有即时通讯应用的对话，“这样你就可以在一个地方看到、回复和管理你与家人和朋友的对话，无论他们使用什么应用。”
在安全性方面，您可以一次性授予APP访问特定传感器(如麦克风、摄像头或位置)的权限。例如，在一段较长的时间后，这些将自动重置，这样您就不会意外地允许访问视频。Android Go 11还可以使用最新手机上的额外屏幕空间，允许基于手势的导航进入主屏幕，向后导航，并在应用程序之间切换。
去年，谷歌表示，500家制造商推出了1600多款Android(Go版)设备，其中三星、小米、Tecno Spark 2和其他公司都很受欢迎。其中许多都不到100美元，还有一些像Tecno Spark Go 2020已经配备了2 GB的内存。Android 11(Go版)将于下个月开始推出。
note_en:
Now that  Android 11 has dropped, Google has unveiled  Android 11 Go Edition with the aim of speeding up entry-level smartphones. Many of the changes are designed to take advantage of improved features on modern budget devices like larger screens, dual cameras, more RAM and extra storage .
 The OS now supports 2GB of RAM (up from 1.5GB before), giving users an additional 270MB of free space — enough to run “three to four more apps in the background,” according to Google. To that end, it can launch apps 20 percent faster than  Android Go 10, allowing for faster app-switching without slowing down your phone. Android Go edition on 2GB devices also supports up to 900MB of additional storage, enough to download a movie, for instance.
  The updated OS also has new convenience and privacy features. It shows conversations from all your messaging apps in a dedicated space in the notification section, “so you can see, respond to, and manage your conversations with family and friends all in one place, no matter what apps they use,” Google wrote.
 In terms of security, you can give one-time permissions to grant app access to specific sensors like your microphone, camera or location. After an extended period of time, those will be auto-reset so that you don’t accidentally allow video access, for example. Android Go 11 can also use the extra screen space found on the latest phones, allowing gesture-based navigation to go to the home screen, navigate backward and switch between apps.
 Last year, Google said that 500 manufacturers launched more than 1,600 Android (Go edition) devices, with popular models from Samsung, Xiaomi, Tecno Spark 2 and others. Many of those cost less than $100, and some like the  Tecno Spark Go 2020 already come with 2GB of RAM. Android 11 (Go edition) will start rolling out next month.
title: 谷歌发布了Android 11 Go Edition，它支持2 GB内存的手机，高于之前的1.5 GB，并表示启动应用程序的速度比Android Go 10快20%
title_en: Google unveils Android 11 Go Edition, which supports phones with 2GB of RAM, up from 1.5GB before, and says it can launch apps 20% faster than Android Go 10

忍者在与Twitch的多年协议中独家回归Twitch。这对泰勒“忍者”布莱文斯来说是另一个重大举措，他已经有了相当一年的时间，即使不考虑正在进行的大流行。
去年8月，他离开Twitch，与Mixer达成独家协议-Mixer于6月底关闭，为了Facebook游戏的零部件而报废。他离开废墟时的状态比现场其他同龄人要好得多(除了迈克尔·“裹尸布”格泽谢克)，据报道，他的口袋里装着他的七位数合同的全部金额。今天，布莱文斯回到了特维奇的家中。
这是一件大事，尤其是因为在他去年有点无礼地离职后，他的频道处理不当。(他们不知何故在上面放了色情内容？)。布莱文斯可能是流媒体领域最知名的人物，他通过与阿迪达斯(Adidas)达成协议，完全进入单一文化主流，他在阿迪达斯有一双鞋，他的商品在沃尔玛(Walmart)等地销售。(他对堡垒之夜也有好感。)。
通过回到特维奇，布莱文斯结束了他下一步可能降落在哪里的问题。作为该网站的前面孔之一，他肯定会再次登上Twitch排行榜的榜首。但这一次，似乎，这将是他自己的条件。他的第一个节目今天上线。
note_en:
Ninja’s back on Twitch, exclusively,  in a multiyear deal with the site. It’s another big move for Tyler “Ninja” Blevins, who’s had quite a year even aside from the ongoing pandemic.
 Last August, he left Twitch for an exclusive deal with Mixer —  which shut down at the end of June, scrapped for parts over at Facebook Gaming. He walked away from the wreckage in much better shape than the rest of his peers on the site (save for Michael “Shroud” Grzesiek), reportedly with the full amount of his seven-figure contract in his pockets. And today, Blevins comes home to Twitch.
  It’s a big deal, not least because of how his channel was mishandled after his somewhat unceremonious departure last year. ( They somehow showed porn on it?) Blevins is probably the most recognizable figure in streaming, having crossed fully over into the monoculture mainstream through deals with Adidas, where he has a shoe, and having his merch sold at places like Walmart. (He also has a skin in  Fortnite.)
 By returning to Twitch, Blevins puts an end to the question of where he might land next. As one of the former faces of the site, he’s sure to top the Twitch charts again. But this time, it seems, it’ll be on his own terms. His first stream goes live today.
title: 泰勒·“忍者”·布莱文斯于2019年8月离开Twitch，与Mixer签订了独家协议，现在又在Twitch上独家回归，与该网站签订了多年的协议
title_en: Tyler “Ninja” Blevins, who left Twitch for an exclusive deal with Mixer in August 2019, is back on Twitch, exclusively, in a multiyear deal with the site

亚马逊(Amazon)聘请前白宫官员彼得·马奎斯(Peter Marquez)担任亚马逊网络服务公司(Amazon Web Services)有史以来第一位空间政策主管，这家科技巨头正在扩大其在太空行业的存在。
周三，马奎兹在LinkedIn上写道，太空社区正在发生许多令人兴奋的事情，这将是一件令人敬畏的事情。
马奎兹是乔治·W·布什和巴拉克·奥巴马政府时期白宫国家安全委员会的空间政策主任。在担任这一职务期间，他领导起草了一份美国空间政策文件，该文件强调国际合作，关注日益严重的空间碎片问题，并阻止天基武器的发展。
在过去的十年里，马奎兹是轨道科学公司(后来被诺斯罗普·格鲁曼公司收购)和行星资源公司(一家现已倒闭的小行星采矿初创公司)的副总裁。他还曾担任Netflix的喜剧“太空力量”(Space Force)系列的太空顾问。
马奎兹加入AWS之际，身家数万亿美元的科技巨头杰夫·贝佐斯(Jeff Bezos)正在扩大其在太空行业的影响力和服务，最近成立了AWS航空航天和卫星解决方案部门。此外，亚马逊在7月份表示，计划向其柯伊伯项目(Project Kuiper)投资100亿美元，这是一个由数千颗互联网卫星组成的雄心勃勃的网络，将与埃隆·马斯克(Elon Musk)的SpaceX Starlink展开直接竞争。

note_en:
Amazon recently hired Peter Marquez as its first-ever head of space policy at Amazon Web Services.
 Marquez was director of space policy for the White House's National Security Council under the Bush 43 and Obama administrations.
 Marquez joins AWS as Jeff Bezos' trillion-dollar tech giant expands its influence and services in the space industry, most recently with the establishment of the AWS Aerospace and Satellite Solutions division.
 Amazon has hired former White House official Peter Marquez to be its first-ever head of space policy at Amazon Web Services as the tech giant expands its presence in the space industry.
 "There are many exciting things happening in the space community and this is going to be awesome," Marquez wrote in a post on LinkedIn on Wednesday.
   Marquez was director of space policy for the White House's National Security Council under the administrations of Presidents George W. Bush and Barack Obama. In that role, he led the drafting of a U.S. space policy document that emphasized international cooperation, gave attention to the growing problem of space debris and discouraged space-based weapons development.
 In the past decade, Marquez was a vice president for both Orbital Sciences, which was later acquired by  Northrop Grumman, and Planetary Resources, a now-defunct asteroid mining start-up. He also was a space advisor for  Netflix's "Space Force" comedy series.
 Marquez joins AWS as  Jeff Bezos' trillion-dollar tech giant expands its influence and services in the space industry, most recently with  the establishment of the AWS Aerospace and Satellite Solutions division. Additionally, Amazon in July said it plans to  invest more than $10 billion into its Project Kuiper division, an ambitious network of thousands of internet satellites that is poised to compete directly with  Elon Musk's SpaceX  Starlink.
 Subscribe to CNBC PRO  for exclusive insights and analysis, and live business day programming from around the world.
title: 亚马逊聘请曾在布什和奥巴马政府中担任万洲国际国家安全委员会(National Security Council)太空政策主任的彼得·马奎斯(Peter Marquez)担任AWS太空政策主管
title_en: Amazon hires Peter Marquez, an ex-director of space policy at WH's National Security Council in Bush and Obama administrations, as head of space policy at AWS

杰克·多西(Jack Dorsey)的支付公司正在邀请其他加密货币公司加入其“联盟”，以汇集专利，保护该行业的开源精神。
Square周四表示，它已经启动了所谓的加密货币开放专利联盟(COPA)，这是一个非营利性组织，希望阻止公司将有用的技术锁定在专利中。Square表示，这一做法阻碍了创新。
Square在一份声明中表示，将基础加密货币技术锁定在专利中会扼杀创新和采用；不良行为者对专利的攻击性使用威胁到加密货币技术的发展。
除了公司小心翼翼地用密码保护自己的工作之外，一些公司走得更远，为他们没有开发计划但会阻碍竞争对手研究的想法申请所谓的先发制人专利。
要成为COPA的一部分，成员必须承诺将他们的专利免费提供给使用共享库的所有其他成员。
Square表示，这个图书馆将作为集体盾牌，保护会员免受专利侵略者的侵害。Square于2018年冒险进入密码领域，并已承诺将自己的密码专利投入新图书馆。
2016年至2017年，美国的加密货币和区块链相关专利数量翻了一番；去年，微软为一种体力消耗驱动的挖掘系统申请了专利，IBM申请了一种具有自我意识的令牌。
假设专利库增长并积聚动力，想法是越来越多的公司将寻求加入COPA以获得技术创新-创造一个更公平的专利环境。
任何从事密码工作的公司，无论是否拥有专利，都有资格加入COPA。
一位发言人证实，Copa将是一个完全独立于Square的实体，有自己的独立董事会。

note_en:
Jack Dorsey’s payment company is inviting other cryptocurrency firms to join its “alliance” to pool patents and preserve the industry’s open-source spirit.
 Square said Thursday it has launched what it calls the  Cryptocurrency Open Patent Alliance (COPA), a non-profit that wants to stop companies from locking up useful technologies in patents, a practice Square says hamstrings innovation.
 "Locking up foundational cryptocurrency technologies in patents stifles innovation and adoption; and offensive use of patents by bad actors threatens the growth of cryptocurrency technologies," Square said in a statement.
 In addition to companies jealously guarding their own work in crypto, some firms go further, filing what are called "pre-emptive patents" for ideas they have no plans of developing but which act to hinder the research of competitors.
 To become part of COPA, members must pledge to make their patents freely available to all other members using a shared library.
 This library will act as a "collective shield" protecting members from "patent aggressors," said Square – which ventured into crypto in 2018 and has already committed to putting its own crypto patents into the new library.
  The number of cryptocurrency and blockchain-related patents in the U.S. doubled between 2016 and 2017; in the past year, Microsoft filed a patent for a mining system  powered by physical exertion and IBM for a token that is a  "self-aware."
 Assuming the patent library grows and gathers momentum, the idea is that more and more companies will look to join COPA to access tech innovations – creating a more equitable patent environment.
 Any company that works in crypto, regardless of whether it has patents or not, will be eligible to join COPA.
 COPA will be an entirely separate entity from Square, with its own independent board of directors, a spokesperson confirmed.
